item.title: Leading contenders emerge for multibillion-dollar deals on disputed Middle East gas project
item.description: Dorra offshore gas field being developed by joint venture comprising subsidiaries of Aramco and Kuwait Petroleum Corporation (KPC)

item.title: Schedule set for ConocoPhillips’ potential buy-out of Australian asset
item.description: Independent expert appointed to value offshore stake

item.title: Australian shale player completes merger with compatriot
item.description: Tamboran Resources expands its acreage and plans for at least four wells to be drilled this year

item.title: Criterium re-awards pipeline gig to keep gas development on track
item.description: Olindo comes on board at Southeast Mengoepeh in Indonesia, after prior deficiencies derail start-up to Q3

item.title: Seatrium in talks with SBM over potential future FPSOs work
item.description: Leading FPSO contractors mulling future workscopes despite Seatrium missing out on the SEAP 1 engineering, procurement and construction prize

item.title: SBM Offshore wins multibillion-dollar FPSO contracts for Petrobras project
item.description: Deals for the P-87 and P-81 floaters destined for the Sergipe-Alagoas basin are worth around $7.8 billion

item.title: Polish LNG terminal appoints commercial and operational advisor
item.description: Lithuania's operator KN Energies secures contract linked to first phase of Gdansk LNG

item.title: Kazakhstan postpones maintenance at second largest oilfield
item.description: Decision to delay Kashagan turnaround coincides with another drop in production at Kazakhstan's largest oilfield Tengiz

item.title: Lengthy West Africa farm-out deal inches closer to completion
item.description: Equatorial Guinea approves sell-down but Chinese endorsement still needed

item.title: BP's winning streak broken with chairman's red card
item.description: Return of some stability to the supermajor after a period of upheaval is interrupted by another high profile departure

item.title: Equinor dishes out contracts for field development
item.description: Baker Hughes extends two oilfield services contracts while DeepOcean secures subsea work for multiple fields including Johan Castberg

item.title: Chinese contractor installs key Aramco offshore facility
item.description: Offshore facility is part of Aramco’s contract release purchase order (CRPO) 149, which was awarded to COOEC in 2024

item.title: Oslo-listed drillers flying high as investor appetite returns
item.description: Norwegian market is getting behind eclectic group of offshore drillers

item.title: ExxonMobil settles coastal lawsuits with US state
item.description: Louisiana’s Jeff Landry says US supermajor agreed to help plug orphaned wells

item.title: Chinese state giant starts full production at heavy oil field
item.description: Hong Kong-listed company says Kenli 10-2 field producing more than 20,500 barrels per day of oil

item.title: This week's leading oil and gas appointments
Medco Energi advancing latest Indonesia gas development
item.description: Gas sales agreement signed and key tender launched for Sakakemang

item.title: Subsea contractors to jointly pursue Australia projects
item.description: Reach Subsea and Beacon Offshore will collaborate Down Under

item.title: Adnoc opens bidding for $5 billion-plus offshore megaproject
item.description: Abu Dhabi state giant recently outlined plans to award 200 billion dirhams ($55 billion) worth of projects from 2026 to 2028, speeding up its spending drive

item.title: Operator launches tender for historic FPSO upgrade
item.description: Finder Energy seeking contractors for modification and life extension works on Petrojarl I

item.title: Duo to develop Malaysia LNG import project
item.description: LNG Alliance and Green Oscasaba team up for 1.2 million tpa scheme

item.title: Cheniere awards $4.69 billion EPC contract for latest expansion project
item.description: Bechtel tapped to add three liquefaction trains at Sabine Pass

item.title: Chevron chief slams potential windfall taxes on oil profits amid Middle East war price surge
item.description: Mike Wirth: New levies ‘would discourage investment at a time when the market signal should be encouraging investment’

item.title: Oil markets reaching ‘unheard-of inventory levels’: ExxonMobil executive
item.description: Senior VP Neil Chapman says supplies may only hold off bigger price shocks for a matter of weeks

item.title: Dismissed BP chair hits out at ‘false allegations’ around his exit
item.description: Albert Manifold claims he was ‘removed without warning or explanation’

item.title: Turkish minister 'optimistic' about Black Sea exploration well
item.description: High-profile Eflani-1 probe could open up new Black Sea play

item.title: Iraq's new oil minister pledges better terms for international investment
item.description: Bassim Mohammed Khudair eyes new oil and gas developments as key to economic growth

item.title: Invictus stock price up after delayed production deal signed in Zimbabwe
item.description: Petroleum Production Sharing Agreement will govern all upstream activities in Cabora Bassa acreage, including development

item.title: Historic EU gas storage lows raise winter energy security concerns
item.description: EU gas storage levels currently around 30% below their five-year average

item.title: Petrobras studying potential tenders for two FPSO revitalisation projects
item.description: Brazilian oil giant assessing alternatives for fields in the Santos and Campos basins
